purpose: Define the controlling scope, methodology, architectural boundaries and working rules for the Inside Rails horse-racing database project.
key_decisions: Preserve all raw source files unchanged.
Treat the downloaded dataset as several related but separate data products.
Use raceform.db as the first source product for structural investigation.
Profile source data before cleaning, integration or target-schema design.
Understand racing-domain meaning before transforming values.
Do not blindly impute missing values, remove duplicate-looking rows or treat outliers as errors.
Test source identifiers, business keys and joins rather than assuming they are valid.
Use conceptual raw, staging, core and analytical layers.
Keep the final database platform open until profiling provides sufficient evidence.
Use Jupyter notebooks as the publishable project audit trail.
open_decisions: Physical implementation of staging, core and analytical layers
Expected grain of each core table
Integration rules for other supplied source products

next_notebook.title: Source Database Structure Profile
next_notebook.purpose: Inspect the supplied raceform.db structure and metadata without cleaning, transforming or redesigning the source data.
